PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

Fixes bug in #7083","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7790/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":1,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7790/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2523541807","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7790#issuecomment-2523541807","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7790","id":2523541807,"node_id":"IC_kwDOACEKh86WajEv","user":{"login":"wmoapl","id":144060883,"node_id":"U_kgDOCJYx0w","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/144060883?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl","html_url":"https://github.com/wmoapl","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-06T15:49:32Z","updated_at":"2024-12-06T15:49:32Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Awesome! Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

Fixes bug in #7083","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7790/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":1,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7790/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2465825191","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7790#issuecomment-2465825191","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7790","id":2465825191,"node_id":"IC_kwDOACEKh86S-YGn","user":{"login":"wmoapl","id":144060883,"node_id":"U_kgDOCJYx0w","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/144060883?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl","html_url":"https://github.com/wmoapl","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-08T22:08:17Z","updated_at":"2024-11-08T22:08:17Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hi there, apologies for the slow progress on this PR. (To be used as a flag to other maintainers)"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-08-30T17:32:17Z","updated_at":"2024-09-03T18:22:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/pulls/7790","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7790","diff_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7790.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7790.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"## PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

Fixes bug in #7083","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7790/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7790/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2327152205","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7790#issuecomment-2327152205","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7790","id":2327152205,"node_id":"IC_kwDOACEKh86KtYZN","user":{"login":"wmoapl","id":144060883,"node_id":"U_kgDOCJYx0w","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/144060883?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl","html_url":"https://github.com/wmoapl","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-03T18:22:31Z","updated_at":"2024-09-03T18:22:31Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hi @nabobalis - keywords can already be passed down to `astropy`, but the issue was that sunpy's internal parsing of paths forces it to be a local path (using `pathlib`) even if the path was a URI.

Though you do not need `fsspec` just to check if the input was a URI, I think using `fsspec` makes sense since it mimics the functionalities that are already supported for local paths, like passing directories and glob characters.

PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

Factories","user":{"login":"wmoapl","id":144060883,"node_id":"U_kgDOCJYx0w","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/144060883?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl","html_url":"https://github.com/wmoapl","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

+@pytest.mark.remote_data
+def test_uri_pattern():
+    """

PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.

{"action":"opened","number":7790,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/pulls/7790","id":2046930170,"node_id":"PR_kwDOACEKh856Aaz6","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7790","diff_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7790.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7790.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7790","number":7790,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"URI handling in Map and Timeseries Factories","user":{"login":"wmoapl","id":144060883,"node_id":"U_kgDOCJYx0w","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/144060883?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl","html_url":"https://github.com/wmoapl","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wmoapl/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description

Adds ability in Map and Timeseries factories to recognize and parse URI paths. It was designed with s3 paths in mind but would work for any type of URI. If path is a URI, it will use the `fsspec` package to open, read, and look for files.

#### Example code:
```
### Reads in fits file from public s3 repository using anonymous credentials
from sunpy.map import Map
Map("s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/sdo/aia/20100513/0131/sdo_aia_h2_20100513T000400_0131_v1.fits", fsspec_kwargs={'anon': True})

### Reads in cdf file from public s3 repository (REQUIRES VALID AWS CREDENTIALS AND SETUP)
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
TimeSeries('s3://gov-nasa-hdrl-data1/cdaweb/ace/mag/level_2_cdaweb/mfi_k2/2017/ac_k2_mfi_20170705_v03.cdf')
```

#### Descriptions to changes in code:

- `Pyproject.toml` - adds `fsspec` as package dependency; adds `s3fs` as test dependency.
- `sunpy/io/_cdf.py` - passes kwargs to reader function.
- `Sunpy/io/_filetools.py` - adds way to open URI filetype using `fsspec.open` to `detect_filetype`
- `Sunpy/map/map_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using `parse_uri` function
- `sunpy/map/tests/test_map_factory.py` - adds unit test to read map from s3 public repository.
- `sunpy/timeseries/tests/test_timeseries_factory.py` - adds unit test to read timeseries from s3 public repository; will skip if credentials/permissions are incorrect.
- `Sunpy/timeseries/timeseries_factory.py` - adds logic to separate URI file path from regular filepath and parses URIs using parse_uri function; refactors previous code for parsing timeseries results into separate function.
- `Sunpy/util/io.py` 
  - `parse_uri` - reads in a list of fsspec OpenFile objects; follows same structure as parse_path
  - `is_uri` - function to recognize URI objects; code taken from `pandas`

Happy to iterate or discuss further on methodology.
